name: Leiothrichidae
description: Leiothrichidae is a large and diverse family of passerine birds found primarily in Asia. They include the laughingthrushes, mesias, minlas, and sibias, known for their often colorful plumage and loud, musical calls.

children.name: Pterorhinus
children.description: A genus of medium to large laughingthrushes with varied plumage, found across East and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pterorhinus pectoralis
children.description: The Greater Necklaced Laughingthrush has grey upperparts, white underparts, and a bold black necklace across the breast. It is a highly social bird found in hill forests of Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pterorhinus chinensis
children.description: The Black-throated Laughingthrush has a black throat and face, chestnut nape, and olive-brown body. It inhabits evergreen forests from southern China to Indochina and Thailand.

children.name: Garrulax
children.description: A genus of striking laughingthrushes with bold head patterns, found in Asia.

children.name: Garrulax leucolophus
children.description: The White-crested Laughingthrush is a striking bird with a white crest and head, black mask, and chestnut-brown body. It is a noisy, social species found in forests from the Himalayas to Sumatra.

children.name: Leiothrix
children.description: A genus of brightly colored, small to medium-sized birds with melodious songs, found in Asia.

children.name: Leiothrix argentauris
children.description: The Silver-eared Mesia is a colorful bird with a bright red bill, silver ear patches, and yellow and olive plumage. It forages in the understory of montane forests from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Leiothrix lutea
children.description: The Red-billed Leiothrix is a small, brightly colored bird with a red bill, yellow throat, and olive-green body. It is a popular cage bird native to the Himalayas and southern China, with introduced populations in Hawaii and Japan.

children.name: Actinodura
children.description: A genus of small to medium-sized birds including minlas and barwings, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Actinodura cyanouroptera
children.description: The Blue-winged Minla is a small, colorful bird with blue wings and tail, a rufous crown, and white underparts. It actively forages in mixed-species flocks in montane forests from the Himalayas to Indochina.

children.name: Actinodura egertoni
children.description: The Rusty-fronted Barwing has a warm rufous forehead, barred wings and tail, and olive-brown body. It forages in the understory of montane forests from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Actinodura ramsayi
children.description: The Spectacled Barwing has distinctive white spectacles around the eyes, barred wings, and a rich brown body. It inhabits hill forests of Southeast Asia, often in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Trochalopteron
children.description: A genus of colorful laughingthrushes with scaled or patterned plumage, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Trochalopteron affine
children.description: The Black-faced Laughingthrush has a black face and throat, olive-brown body, and bright rufous wings with white tips. It is a social bird of montane forests from the Himalayas to Indochina.

children.name: Trochalopteron squamatum
children.description: The Blue-winged Laughingthrush has bright blue wings and tail contrasting with a rufous-brown body and blackish face. It inhabits montane forests and scrub from the Himalayas to southern China.

children.name: Trochalopteron subunicolor
children.description: The Scaly Laughingthrush has olive-brown plumage with fine dark scaling on the breast and neck, and rufous-tinged wings. It is found in montane forests of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Trochalopteron ocellatum
children.description: The Spotted Laughingthrush has dark plumage with prominent white spots on the breast and head, and rufous-tinged wings. It inhabits rhododendron and oak forests in the Himalayas.

children.name: Trochalopteron erythrocephalum
children.description: The Chestnut-crowned Laughingthrush has a rich chestnut crown, dark mask, and olive-brown body with rufous wings. It forages in the undergrowth of montane forests from the Himalayas to western China.

children.name: Trochalopteron formosum
children.description: The Red-winged Laughingthrush has striking crimson-red wings and tail, a blackish face, and grey-brown body. It is endemic to montane forests of southern China and northern Vietnam.

children.name: Trochalopteron milnei
children.description: The Red-tailed Laughingthrush has a vivid red tail, olive-brown body, black face, and white streaks on the ear coverts. It inhabits evergreen forests of southern China and adjacent Southeast Asia.

children.name: Heterophasia
children.description: A genus of slender, long-tailed birds with melodious songs, known as sibias, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Heterophasia capistrata
children.description: The Rufous Sibia has warm rufous underparts, a dark cap, and a long graduated tail. It is an active, vocal bird of montane forests in the Himalayas, often in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Heterophasia gracilis
children.description: The Grey Sibia is a sleek, grey-plumaged bird with a black mask, white wing patches, and a long tail. It forages actively in the canopy of montane forests in northeastern India and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Heterophasia pulchella
children.description: The Beautiful Sibia has rich chestnut underparts, a grey head, and blue-grey wings with white patches. It inhabits montane forests of the eastern Himalayas and southern China.

children.name: Cutia
children.description: A genus of strikingly patterned birds with barred underparts, found in montane forests of Asia.

children.name: Cutia nipalensis
children.description: The Nepal Cutia has a striking pattern: chestnut breast band, black barring on white underparts, and blue-grey upperparts. It forages in the canopy of montane forests from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Cutia legalleni
children.description: The Vietnamese Cutia resembles the Nepal Cutia but has a narrower chestnut breast band and more extensive black barring. It is endemic to montane forests of Vietnam and Laos.
